rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Enforce Foreign Subpoena
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Thursday, May 15, 2025, line 1, PETITIONER LYFT, INC.'S Hearing On Petition To Enforce Foreign Subpoena Continued to May 28, 2025, per amended notice of hearing. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: PAC-12 ENTERPRISES, LLC VS. DIRECTORS MORTGAGE, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing For Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Thursday, May 15, 2025, line 2, PETITIONER PAC-12 ENTERPRISES, LLC'S Hearing For Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award The court grants the unopposed motion to approve the Proposition 65 settlement and consent judgment. The court finds pursuant to Health and Safety Code section 25249.7, subdivision (f)(4), that the warning required by the settlement is in compliance with Proposition 65 (Health & Safety Code sections 25249.5 to 25249.14); that the award of attorney's fees pursuant to the settlement is reasonable under California law; and that, considering the factors set out in Health & Safety Code section 25249.7, subdivision (b)(2), and the facts asserted in the moving papers, the penalty amount is reasonable. Accordingly the motion is granted and the parties may submit the proposed judgment for signature. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address.

rulings.Case Title: DEBORAH HIGER VS. DIGNITY HEALTH, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions; Points And Authorities; Declaration Of James C. Ashworth
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Thursday, May 15, 2025, line 4, 1 - PLAINTIFF DEBORAH HIGER'S Motion To Compel Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents And For Monetary Sanctions The court previously heard this motion and set a further briefing and hearing schedule. No party has filed updates. Accordingly, the court assumes that all remaining discovery disputes concerning Plaintiff's motion to compel further responses to her requests for production of documents have been resolved. The court previously put over the issue of sanctions. (See Order, May 7, 2025, at 4:28-5:1.) The court finds that Dignity's opposition to Plaintiff's motion lacked substantial justification in part in its overreading of Evidence Code 1157 but reduces the sanctions award to reflect that the parties have resolved their remaining disputes concerning this motion. The court awards $1000 in sanctions, payable by Dignity Health to Plaintiff, within 30 days of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. rulings.Case Title: JANITA MARIE SAM VS. GLORIA SCHLIESSER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Thursday, May 15, 2025, line 9, CROSS DEFENDANT JULIO BERMUDEZ'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Monetary Sanctions The unopposed motion by cross-defendant Julio Bermudez for further responses to his form interrogatories, set one, and requests for production of documents to plaintiff Janita Marie Sam and for monetary sanctions is granted. Sam shall provide further responses to form interrogatories 6.3(b), 6.4(d), 6.5(d-e), 6.7(c), 20.2(g), 20.3, and 20.8(a-c) within 15 days of entry of this order. Sam shall produce documents responsive to request for production numbers 1, 9, 10, 11, 12, 15, 20, 21, 23, 24, and 25 within 15 days of entry of this order. In addition, Sam's responses to the requests for production are incomplete (for instance, where she represents that she does not have documents, she must include the information set out in CCP 2031.230). Sam shall provide further responses to request for production numbers 2-8, 13-14, 16-19, 22, and 32 that are code compliant within 15 days of entry of this order. Sanctions are appropriate on a discovery motion where the objecting party does not act with substantial justification in its discovery compliance, even where she does not oppose the motion. (See CCP 2029.290(c); id. 2031.300(c); Rule of Court 3.1348(a).) The court awards sanctions but reduces the amount to reflect hours billed less the additional $1000 that Bermudez seeks for CCP 2023.050(a) and to reflect that no reply brief was needed. The court orders Sam to pay sanctions of $3724 to Bermudez within 10 court days of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: FANX, INC. VS. JAMES DAVIS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Thursday, May 15, 2025, line 10, DEFENDANTS JAMES DAVIS, SAMANTHA DAVIS DOOLITTLE'S Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery The unopposed motion by defendants and cross-complainants Samantha Davis Doolittle and James Davis for responses to discovery, a deemed-admitted order, and sanctions against cross-defendant Andrew Wong is granted. The Millar declaration establishes that the Davises served form interrogatories, requests for production of documents, and requests for admissions and Wong has not responded. By failing to respond he has waived objections. (See CCP 2030.290(a), 2031.300(a), 2033.280(a).) The court orders Wong to serve verified, objection-free responses to the form interrogatories and requests for production within 15 days of entry of this order. The matters subject to the requests for admission are deemed admitted by Wong. (CCP 2033.280(b), (c).) The Davises seek sanctions. Sanctions are appropriate on a discovery motion where the objecting party does not act with substantial justification in its discovery compliance, even where she does not oppose the motion. (See CCP 2029.290(c); id. 2031.300(c); Rule of Court 3.1348(a).) Sanctions are mandatory where a party fails to respond to requests for admission. (CCP 2033.280(c).) The court orders Wong to pay sanctions but reduces the amount to reflect that no reply brief was needed. Wong shall pay $1060 to the Davises within 15 days of entry of this order. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)

rulings.Case Title: TOWERVIEW SF II, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. BARTHOLOMEW MURPHY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants (1) Vanguard Properties, Inc., (2) Andrew Warren, And (3) Frank Nolan'S Compliance With Court Order And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Law & Motion/Discovery Calendar for Thursday, May 15, 2025, line 12, PLAINTIFF TOWERVIEW SF II, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY CORPORATION'S Motion To Compel Defendants (1) Vanguard Properties, Inc., (2) Andrew Warren, And (3) Frank Nolan'S Compliance With Court Order And For Monetary Sanctions The court orders the Vanguard Defendants (Andrew Warren, Frank Nolan, and Vanguard Properties Inc.) to provide verified responses to plaintiff Towerview SF II LLC's Requests for Production of Documents within 10 days of entry of this order that exclude any objections based on joint defense privilege, lack of notice to consumers, or equal availability of documents to the plaintiff, by May 22, 2025. The Vanguard Defendants are ordered to provide a privilege log and all responsive documents by May 29, 2025. The Vanguard Defendants' failure to comply with this court's discovery order lacks substantial justification and subsequent noncompliance may subject these defendants to evidentiary or issue sanctions. The court orders the defendants to pay $5000 to Towerview SF II, LLC in sanctions by May 29, 2025. The court does not award the full sanctions amount requested because Towerview did not obtain all the relief it sought and because Towerview did not break out its counsel's time between time spent on the motion (necessary to enforce discovery obligations) and time spent meeting and confer (an anticipated aspect of the discovery process). Each of the Vanguard Defendants is jointly and severally liable for the sanctions payment. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)

The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. = (301/CVA)
